author: mark
text:
Hard-fail bl prime when master_url is unreachable

Roll back the silent local-orphan fallback in state_repo::ensure for first-time setup. A master_url client is a pure pointer to a shared hub; falling through to an isolated local store on an unreachable URL lets the user drift away from the team — the failure mode the cross-repo model exists to prevent.

ensure() now returns Err on first-time fetch failure (tearing down the just-initialized scaffold), naming the URL, the underlying git stderr, and the three resolution paths. Warm-cache offline keeps soft-failing with a note. auto_provision_master propagates the error so bl prime surfaces it verbatim; bl init already does.

Detach is the escape hatch, so it must work offline — added remaster::try_cold_detach: when master_url is set and state-repo has not materialized, clear master_url/state_remote in committed config and initialize the legacy .balls/worktree layout, no network. The warm detach path is unchanged when the state-repo exists.
